response.justification: Candidate has strong senior IC experience (13+ years) across diverse stacks and a clear interest in developer tooling, testing, and craftsmanship (TDD workshops, test runner, assertion library). However, the role is a people-management position requiring 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years of technical leadership specifically in developer tools/build systems/test automation at scale, plus mobile-platform familiarity. The resume shows no people-management history (no direct reports, no team-lead titles, no performance review or hiring responsibilities), and developer-tooling work appears as side projects rather than a primary professional focus. Mobile experience is limited to a 2015–2016 iOS/tvOS stint. Fit for a Staff IC role on a tooling team is plausible, but not for an Engineering Manager.
strengths: Deep generalist engineering background with strong testing/TDD and craftsmanship culture
Has built developer tooling artifacts (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD infra, MCP code generators)
Demonstrated teaching/mentorship via workshops, dojos, and multi-module courses
concerns: No evidence of formal people management (no direct reports, 1:1s, hiring, or perf reviews)
Mobile-platform ecosystem experience is thin and dated (single iOS/tvOS role ~2015–2016)
Developer-tools work is mostly side-project scope, not large-scale build/test infrastructure leadership
